svg.text: Anecdotes, cherry-picked facts, and weak analogies get dressed up as solid support.
Start with the concrete case: Anecdotes, cherry-picked facts, and weak analogies get dressed up as solid support.
Name the hidden structure: Evidence integrity meter from cherry-pick to representative evidence.
Watch for the trap: Mistaking selected evidence for balanced evidence.
Transfer the habit immediately: Take one cherry-picked claim and rebuild it using fuller evidence.

Unlike broad intro courses, this lesson turns evidence fallacies into a practical operating skill through a real scenario, a visual model, a retrieval gate, and a transfer task built around argument mapping, evidence discipline, and fair-minded judgment.
Mistaking selected evidence for balanced evidence.

Take one cherry-picked claim and rebuild it using fuller evidence.
The goal is not to memorize the label, but to practice a repeatable move in real judgment. Ask the learner to produce a concrete artifact, not just an opinion.
